title: UX Library
intro: A composable Android UI layer on top of NavSDK that helps you build driving applications — Automotive AAOS, Logistics, Fleet — without writing a full navigation UI from scratch.

what.heading: What is UX Library?
what.body1: UX Library is TomTom's customisation layer for navigation applications. It sits between NavSDK (the routing, map rendering and positioning engine) and the OEM application, providing production-ready UI components, design tokens, and a stable API surface for tailoring every visible aspect of the navigation experience.
what.body2: OEMs get a coherent, ANA-consistent baseline out of the box. From there, every layer is overridable — colours, typography, layout, component structure, and even core functional components like the search engine — without forking or maintaining a parallel UI codebase.

map.title: Map as wallpaper
map.body: The navigation map fills the entire IVI home screen as a persistent background layer.

theme.title: Full theming
theme.body: Override any design token — colour, radius, typography — to match OEM brand guidelines.

modules.title: Replaceable modules
modules.body: Swap search, voice, or routing providers without changing any UI code.

layouts.title: Composable layouts
layouts.body: Resize the navigation area from any edge. Other domain widgets layer on top.

explore.heading: Explore this library
explore.subheading: Key capabilities of the UX Library — click any card to go to the full documentation.

callout: This is a prototype. Pages with full interactive content are Colour, Design Tokens, Font, Corner radius, Home Screen Layout, Search engine, Navigation Controls, Horizon Panel, Next Instruction Panel, ETA Panel, Route Bar, TomTom AI Assistant, and EV Support. All other nav items are placeholders to be built out.
